creator: mmokhi@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to kennedy.greg from comment #4)
I found the reason of problem.

In brief it's because the MySQL developers didn't considered NO_SSL option for WITH_SSL=no in a CMake script [ssl.cmake] (i think maybe in new revision of >= 5.7.10 at least, perhaps).

I'm talking with them.
Maybe we can do patch in CMake script (and some of cpp codes) to return that option back again. Or i have to delete that option.

Notice, NO_SSL means not use ssl in source code building at all (and is different from NOT USING ssl).
you can build with SSL support and don't use it with --ssl=0 in server side, --ssl-mode=DISABLED in client.

I'll get back to you with more news (and/or patches) tomorrow (in my time ;D)

Regards, Mokhi.
